title: First Solar Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: First Solar reported exceptional Q1 2026 results with record revenue of $1.0 billion, representing 24% year-over-year growth. The company achieved a 47% gross margin, expanding six percentage points compared to 2025, driven by higher Section 45X tax credit volumes and reduced freight costs. Net income reached $347 million with diluted EPS of $3.22, a 65% increase from the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA totaled $520 million with a 50% margin, exceeding the guidance range's upper end. The company produced 4.3 GW of modules, with approximately 3 GW from U.S. facilities operating at 96% utilization. India deliveries totaled 1 GW at $0.20/watt average selling price, while U.S. bookings of 0.9 GW averaged $0.34/watt. The CURE technology launch completed successfully at the Perrysburg facility, with the first Series 6 line ramping as expected. This rollout could unlock approximately $600 million in additional revenue through technology adjusters across 2027-2028. The contracted backlog reached 47.9 GW valued at $14.4 billion, with deliveries scheduled through 2030, providing substantial visibility. Management maintained unchanged full-year 2026 guidance with Q2 expectations of 3.4-4.0 GW volumes and $400-500 million adjusted EBITDA. The South Carolina finishing facility remains on track for 2026 production start, while the perovskite pilot line is scheduled for 2027 launch.
